title: eremophila subfloccosa subsp. subfloccosa
intro: Eremophila subfloccosa subsp. subfloccosa, also known as the Fleshy Emu Bush, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Scrophulariaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which help to soothe and protect the skin and mucous membranes. The health benefits of Eremophila subfloccosa subsp. subfloccosa include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and combat infections. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and ointments for topical application, making it a popular natural remedy for skin issues and minor ailments.
